engine: fak in-kernel CUDA serve (fak serve --gguf ... --backend cuda)
category: real fak in-kernel device decode of a real 0.5B Q8 checkpoint; NOT a 753B serving rate, NOT a GPU server2/A100 number, NOT a stock-engine comparison
binary: fak-gpu:latest image built 2026-06-21T19:24Z (predates the devMu fix); single-stream numbers are unaffected by the fix

single_stream_decode.note: warm steady-state; the very first decode after a cold container start measures ~66 tok/s (includes CUDA-context + first-graph warmup) and must not be quoted as the steady-state rate

concurrency.observed: a 2-way (and 4-way) concurrent /v1/messages burst returns 0 usable tokens: one stream drops the connection (http 000), the other returns garbage ('!!!!'), and the CUDA context is poisoned with thousands of sticky 'fak-cuda: cuda_kernels.cu:81/:88 an illegal memory access was encountered' errors until the container is restarted
concurrency.root_cause: the in-kernel planner did not serialize the whole device forward pass: the single-stream CUDA backend (one g_stream, one cuBLAS handle, a shared device free-list) was driven by two concurrent agent.InKernelPlanner.Complete goroutines whose per-token op sequences interleaved and corrupted each other's device buffers
concurrency.fix: internal/agent/inkernel_planner.go: hold a dedicated devMu across the entire forward when backend != nil, serializing concurrent device requests into safe queuing (correct for a single-stream device). Paired GPU-free honesty test TestInKernelConcurrentDeviceCompleteSerializes (proven non-vacuous: 3719 overlaps without the lock, 0 with it).
concurrency.fix_status: in tree; NOT yet redeployed to the live fak-gpu container (the CUDA image build pipeline is out-of-band of this repo)

contrast.cpu_serve_8081: the sibling CPU GGUF serve (qwen2.5-0.5b-cpu, same model, --backend omitted) answered correctly throughout (http 200, real text), isolating the fault to the CUDA backend path
